mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1.0% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.65% as of 2020-03-15 indicates emergency easing
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative forward returns as investors reprrice growth expectations downward over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% as of 2020-03-15 indicates labor stress
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or the mechanism produces positive returns in more than 45% of cases when UNRATE > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate earnings expectations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when sales fall below the prior month level.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 56% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal supply-side capacity constraints when both rise simultaneously. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive equity returns over 60 trading days as supply expansion supports earnings growth without inflation pressure.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both increase > 0.5% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.66 and PAYEMS at 151786 as of 2020-03-15
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) produces no statistically significant improvement over single-series models (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1500 thousand units signals residential demand weakness that predicts broader economic contraction and negative equity returns over 20 trading days. This mechanism reflects forward-looking housing market signals of consumer confidence deterioration.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1599 thousand units as of 2020-02-15
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when HOUST < 1500, or the mechanism fails to predict returns better than random baseline (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate jointly determine real monetary conditions. When FEDFUNDS falls below CPIAUCSL growth rate, real rates turn negative, signaling monetary accommodation that drives positive equity returns over 60 trading days through asset price inflation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.65% and CPIAUCSL at 257.953 as of 2020-03-15
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 57% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month signals weakening labor demand that predicts earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 56% of triggered instances, or the mechanism produces positive returns in more than 48% of cases when PAYEMS growth < 0.2%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) serves as a real-time supply capacity indicator. Sustained INDPRO growth above 0.3% month-over-month signals robust supply-side expansion that supports equity valuations through improved corporate margins and productivity, predicting positive returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 54% of instances when INDPRO growth > 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 12-month window.
